description: Document unused shared components and composables that are defined but not yet integrated. These are not dead code per se (they are likely prepared for future use per DESIGN_SYSTEM.md 'Next UI components to add' section), but they should be documented so the team is aware of what is available vs actively used. Also add a brief note about useVirtualScroll being ready for integration with KDataTable.
steps: Add a COMPONENT_USAGE.md file to panel/web/shared/ that documents: (a) Components currently imported and used: KButton, KChart, KConfirmDialog, KDataTable, KEmptyState, KFormField, KInput, KSelect, KSkeleton, KStatusPill, KTabs, KTextarea, KToast, KAvatar. (b) Components defined but not yet imported: KAlert, KBreadcrumb, KDrawer, KPagination. (c) Composables currently used: useApi, useClipboard, useConfirm, useFormValidation, useI18n, useTheme, useToast, useWebSocket. (d) Composables defined but not yet imported: useVirtualScroll (ready for KDataTable large dataset optimization).

In the same file, note that useVirtualScroll is specifically designed to work with KDataTable for datasets with 1000+ rows (as documented in its JSDoc), and KPagination could be the alternative UX for large tables.
acceptance_criteria: panel/web/shared/COMPONENT_USAGE.md exists with accurate usage status for all shared components and composables
The document distinguishes between 'actively used' and 'available for future integration'
The document is accurate based on the current state of imports across admin and portal
verification: Read panel/web/shared/COMPONENT_USAGE.md and verify it lists all shared components and composables with correct usage status
findings: Created COMPONENT_USAGE.md documenting 14 actively used components, 4 available-for-future components, 10 actively used composables, and 1 available-for-future composable. Includes notes about useVirtualScroll + KDataTable integration and KPagination as an alternative approach for large datasets. References DESIGN_SYSTEM.md roadmap for rationale on keeping unused components.
